tags.name: Punta Dalila
tags.qa_note: Western leeward reef. Confirmed boat entry, beginner-friendly, ~15m (50ft) max depth. Multiple sources confirm gentle current, reef type, good sea turtle and nurse shark sightings. Validated with 4 sources.

tags.name: Cardona
tags.qa_note: Shore dive confirmed. Low-profile broad flat reef full of holes and crevasses; ~14m (45ft) max depth (corrected from 12m). Named after Mexican film director René Cardona who filmed 'Un Mundo Nuevo' here in 1957. Splendid toadfish, nurse sharks, lobsters, crabs, spotted morays confirmed by multiple sources. Good night dive site. Validated with 4 sources.

tags.name: San Frandsco Reef
tags.qa_note: OSM name has typo (San Frandsco). Known as San Francisco Reef or San Francisco Wall in all sources. Wall confirmed by 5 sources.

tags.name: Las Palamas
tags.qa_note: OSM name has typo (Las Palamas). Known as Las Palmas Reef in all sources. Sources indicate beginner-friendly site with max 45ft (14m). Difficulty corrected from Intermediate to Beginner.

tags.name: Maracaibo Reef
tags.qa_note: Southernmost Cozumel dive site near Punta Celerain Lighthouse. Features famous arch at approx 35m (115ft). Wall drops 70-150ft (21-46m). 40m max depth in our data is conservative but within recreational range.

tags.name: El Islote
tags.qa_note: Southeast Cozumel near Punta Celarain lighthouse. Large patch coral island with broad ridges of pristine hard and soft coral separated by sandy channels. Advanced dive with strong current and depth 60-130ft (18-40m); our 24m (80ft) max is within confirmed range. Depth corrected from 20m to 24m; difficulty upgraded from Intermediate to Advanced. Only 2 sources found — not yet validated to 3-source standard.

tags.name: Chun Chacaaab Reef
tags.qa_note: Known as Chun Chacaab in most sources. Southern reef near Punta Sur. Nitrox 32 recommended per Barefoot Cozumel.

tags.name: Chen Rio
tags.qa_note: CORRECTION: Chen Rio is a SHORE dive (not boat). East coast site with sheltered natural bay protected by rocky rim. Small bay inside the reef sheltered from surf; drop-off to deeper water on outside. Max depth ~18m (60ft). Maya name means 'stone mouth'. Entry type corrected from boat to shore. Only 2 sources found — not yet validated to 3-source standard.

tags.name: El Mirador
tags.qa_note: CORRECTIONS: Shore dive (not boat). Max depth ~11m (35ft) per colemanconcierge.com (corrected from 22m). East coast location with multiple small bays; rocky seascape with natural bridges and tide pools. Dangerous undertow can be present. Intermediate difficulty due to variable surf and undertow. Depth and entry type corrected. Only 2 sources found — not validated to 3-source standard.

tags.name: San Juan Reef
tags.qa_note: CORRECTION: Difficulty upgraded from Intermediate to Advanced. North end of Cozumel outside the marine park; extremely strong and unpredictable currents — operators rate as Advanced/Expert. Features finger corals, lettuce coral, barrel sponges, ledges with French grunts, schoolmasters, moray eels, nurse sharks, turtles, eagle rays. Depth 40-60ft (12-18m) confirmed. Often second dive after Barracuda or Cantarel. Risk of separation from group and drift past island noted by multiple sources. Validated with 5 sources.

tags.name: Barracuda Reef
tags.qa_note: Site is a WALL (rocky ledge drop-off), not a flat reef. Real depth range 90-120ft (27-37m). Sources say 'Expert' level due to current 2-10 knots, downcurrents, and risk of being lost at sea. Difficulty is conservatively listed as Advanced; operators should treat as Expert. Port Captain monitors and restricts diving here.

tags.name: Paraiso Reef
tags.qa_note: Site is south of the Puerto Maya cruise ship pier (not north). Named after Rancho Paraiso. Three sections at 5m, 13m, and 18m depth. Strong current warning: proximity to cruise ship pier creates hazard.

tags.name: Villa Blanca Wall
tags.qa_note: Wall starts at 45ft (15m) just south of San Miguel (NOT the northern hotel zone as previously described). Extends north to Cantarel. Currents can be strong and unpredictable. Sources rate as Expert/Advanced due to current risks. Difficulty upgraded from Intermediate to Advanced.

tags.name: Cantarell Sand Reef
tags.qa_note: CRITICAL CORRECTION: This site is actually a WALL (known as 'Cantarel' or 'Spotted Eagle Ray Wall'), not a sand reef. Real depth 40-100ft (12-30m). Strong to extremely strong currents. Known for eagle ray aggregations Dec-Feb. Difficulty corrected from Beginner to Advanced. Depth corrected from 10m to 30m.

tags.name: Wreck C-53
tags.qa_note: Verified: USS Scuffle (AM-298), Admirable-class minesweeper, built 1943 Tampa. 184ft (56m) long, 33ft wide, 40ft high keel-to-superstructure. Scuttled June 2000. Rests upright at 24m near Chankanaab Park (west/southwest coast, NOT northwest). Interior accessible to certified wreck divers.
